title: Questions from Quarantine: Halloween and COVID-19
summary: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine.
body: The Security and Global Health Project is proud to present a weekly web series with Security Mom Juliette Kayyem and Medicine Mom Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux. Each week, our experts will answer your questions from quarantine and give you advice on staying sane and sanitary in a global crisis. We hope you'll join our Moms every Tuesday, and if you have a question that you want answered, tweet with #QuestionsFromQuarantine . _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Security and Global Health Project hosts a weekly web series featuring experts Juliette Kayyem and Dr. Margaret Bourdeaux, who provide advice on managing life during the COVID-19 pandemic. The series, titled 'Questions from Quarantine,' addresses public concerns about staying safe and mentally healthy during the global health crisis. Each episode invites viewers to submit questions via social media using a dedicated hashtag. The specific article references a Halloween-themed episode, indicating a focus on adapting traditional activities like holidays to public health guidelines during the pandemic. The broader context includes related expert analyses on topics such as mass migration, school reopenings, and global security, positioning the series within wider discussions on crisis management and public policy.
result.main_content_zh: 安全与全球健康项目推出每周网络系列节目，由专家朱丽叶·凯耶姆和玛格丽特·布尔多博士主持，为观众提供在COVID-19疫情期间管理生活的建议。该系列名为“隔离中的问题”，旨在解答公众在全球健康危机中如何保持安全与心理健康的疑问。每期节目邀请观众通过社交媒体使用特定标签提交问题。本文提及了万圣节主题的剧集，表明其关注点在于如何使传统节日活动适应疫情期间的公共卫生指南。更广泛的背景包括相关专家对大规模移民、学校重新开放和全球安全等议题的分析，将该系列置于危机管理和公共政策的更广泛讨论中。
